subject: LU-2074 build: fix 'copy into fixed size buffer' errors

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Patch Set 1:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/9622/

Build Failed 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/9622/ : FAILURE

Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

It looks like some place don't know about strlcat. 

from the build fail: 
mount_utils_ldiskfs.c:1045: warning: implicit declaration of function 'strlcat'

Patch Set 1:

This is strange, as I do not see this problem in my own build environment.
To try to fix this issue, I will move the '#include <libcfs/libcfs.h>' line above the '#ifdef __linux__' condition.
Uploaded patch set 2.

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.3/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/bb3bafea-16dd-11e2-962d-52540035b04c).  Ran 12 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 6: (2 inline comments)

Well this is tricky patch for me.   I see the benefit of what it is trying to do but I don't see why the code needs to get harder to read.

For sure I would rather the the null termination be in a macro.  Why some of the spots need null termination but not all of them?

I see that we don't fix all strcpys.  I am sure there is some logic as to why that is.  Can you discuss that some?  Perhaps there are other changes that can be made in these areas that are singled out that will make things overall safer.

Patch Set 6:

Hi Keith,

You are right, the patch does not fix all incorrect strcpy()/strcat() calls. The spots addressed here were found by Coverity, but apparently it missed some others.

Maybe you can help me solve an issue I was not able to cope with. My initial intention was to replace all incorrect calls to strcpy() with a call to strlcpy(), and similarly to replace all incorrect calls to strcat() with a call to strlcat(). Doing so would avoid the need for separately setting the NUL termination (and hence the need for a macro). The problem is I was not able to use strlcpy()/strlcat() in some files, like liblustreapi.c. For a reason I cannot understand, I get a compilation error if I call one of these 2 functions in that file.

Can you see where this problem stems from?

Thanks for your help,
Sebastien.

Patch Set 6:

Let's discuss some of this in the LU.

Patch Set 6:

In my RHEL6.3 build environment I don't have issues with strlcpy.  Can you show the build failure you see?

Patch Set 6:

The error does not occur at compile time, but at link time:
Making all in tests
make[5]: Entering directory `/depot/tmps/buissons/myrpm/BUILD/lustre-2.3.58/lustre/tests'
make[6]: Entering directory `/depot/tmps/buissons/myrpm/BUILD/lustre-2.3.58/lustre/tests'
../../libcfs/libcfs/libcfs.a(libcfs_a-user-prim.o): In function `cfs_create_thread':
/depot/tmps/buissons/myrpm/BUILD/lustre-2.3.58/libcfs/libcfs/user-prim.c:241: undefined reference to `pthread_create'
collect2: ld returned 1 exit status
make[6]: *** [ll_dirstripe_verify] Error 1
make[6]: *** Waiting for unfinished jobs....
make[6]: Leaving directory `/depot/tmps/buissons/myrpm/BUILD/lustre-2.3.58/lustre/tests'
make[5]: *** [all-recursive] Error 1
make[5]: Leaving directory `/depot/tmps/buissons/myrpm/BUILD/lustre-2.3.58/lustre/tests'
make[4]: *** [all-recursive] Error 1


Compared to the patch being inspected, I got this error simply by replacing the call to strncpy() with a call to strlcpy() in the get_param_obdvar() function in lustre/utils/liblustreapi.c.
If I revert back to strncpy(), then it compiles and links successfully.

Patch Set 6:

Thanks for the update.  I will look into this again tomorrow.

Patch Set 6:

I see this same error in some build conditions.  I don't think this is related your specific change but a more general build issue.  I am working on this and will update you when I know a bit more.

Patch Set 6:

Hi,
I will post a new patch including the problematic strlcat/strlcpy calls, so that you can see where it causes trouble in the code, and help resolution of LU-2662.
Uploaded patch set 7.

(14 inline comments)

(3 inline comments)

Patch Set 18:

Patch Set 18: Verified

Patch Set 18:

Hi Dmitry,

I am not sure I understand correctly your comment in file lustre/utils/obd.c. Do you want me to replace all E2BIG occurrences in my patch with another errcode, like ENAMETOOLONG?

Concerning your other remark about nullifying the last char of the strings, it is mandatory when using strncpy(), because this routine does not ensure null termination.

Patch Set 18:

My comment was about positive value of E2BIG. It should be negative -E2BIG. So, I concerned about positives error codes.

Still I don't understand why we need to nullify if we check that buffer is enough. This means that strncpy() will store '\0' there because space in buffer enough (we check this). It's just double nullify. If we don't check the buffer we have to nullify.

Patch Set 18:

OK, I will return -E2BIG. I have also turned into negative the other returns in this function.

Concerning the double nullification, it is not exactly true because I am copying at most sizeof()-1 characters. I will change this to copy sizeof() characters, so that strncpy() nullifies the remainder of the string.
Uploaded patch set 19.

I think both a size check and then strnlen s a bit overkill, but perhaps the tools complain about strcpy as unsafe even after the check?

parents.subject: Revert "LU-3544 nfs: writing to new files will return ENOENT"

commit.subject: LU-2074 build: fix 'copy into fixed size buffer' errors
commit.message:
LU-2074 build: fix 'copy into fixed size buffer' errors

Fix 'copy into fixed size buffer' defects found by Coverity
version 6.0.3:
Copy into fixed size buffer (STRING_OVERFLOW)
The fixed-size string might be overrun by copying without
checking the length.

Signed-off-by: Sebastien Buisson <sebastien.buisson@bull.net>
Change-Id: Ia47e6ae132fe476fce202ce06d6fc655f9855012
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/4154
Reviewed-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Tested-by: Hudson
Tested-by: Maloo <whamcloud.maloo@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
